title: Rebuilding Acme's Logistics Platform
subtitle: A legacy rewrite that cut dispatch time in half
problem:
Acme's dispatch ran on a 12-year-old desktop application that only one vendor could maintain. Orders were keyed in twice, outages were common, and the team had no visibility into driver status.

The business needed to scale into three new markets, but the software could not follow.
solution:
We rebuilt the platform as a cloud-native web application with a real-time dispatch board.

1. Domain model
We started by mapping the order lifecycle with Acme's dispatchers, then modeled it cleanly so the new system matched how the business actually worked.

2. Incremental cutover
We ran the old and new systems in parallel and migrated one market at a time, so dispatch never stopped.
impact: Dispatch time dropped by half and double-entry disappeared. Acme opened two new markets in the first year on the same headcount.

seo.metaDescription: How SEQTEK rebuilt Acme's legacy dispatch system as a cloud-native platform and cut dispatch time in half.
